description.p: Tensaku is a modern screenshot annotation tool for Linux, designed for wlroots-based compositors. It provides a simple, intuitive toolset for annotating screenshots with smooth hardware-accelerated rendering.
Tensaku is a fork of Satty by Matthias Gabriel, used under the MPL-2.0 license.

screenshot.caption: The annotation canvas with the layer panel open — every mark stays selectable, draggable, and re-orderable

ul.li: also try the legacy Hyprland dispatcher for older versions
floor crop-resize width to keep the top bar one row
hold crop/grow window size across moves on Hyprland
arrow-key toolbar nav + Esc cancels crop from any control
aspect-locked arrow resize, sticky control focus, smarter top-bar wrap
view-window crop model, non-destructive transforms, full keyboard nav
session batch — glyph tooltips, Pen/Counter, slider+cursor fixes, group move
match the resize popover's units dropdown padding to the toolbar
drop the single-row floor's +8px buffer to close the tool gap
scrollbars track the crop region, not the full image
plain wheel adjusts the crop; mouse only touches crop handles
tighten the single-row floor to the packed toolbar width
don't show handles for a hidden layer
curve to the top by default
un-invert Shift+arrow resize under a locked aspect ratio
renderer core for materialized-crop model (not yet wired)
dismiss swatch tooltips when the color picker closes
force SpinButton repaint so detected scale pre-fills
report envs.conf wiring, not just the live env
accurate Omarchy reporting
--wire-omarchy also floats + centers the Tensaku window
auto-install and wire the Omarchy screenshot wrapper
correct screenshot sizing & sharpness on fractional-scale monitors
add crate-level doc comment to main.rs
